THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF L'ABBE, DANIEL RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT NOWELL, AERON AERON NOWELL AS THE ADMINSTRATOR OF THE ESTATE OF KAREN W HOKE $23,383.00 PRINCIPAL, $1,095.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $24,478.00, SEE SCANNED DOC - "PRINCIPAL" IS COMPRISED OF THE FOLLOWING: $20,000.00 FOR LOSS OF USE AND ENJOYMENT OF PLAINTIFF'S PROPERTY, AND RELATED EMOTIONAL AND MENTAL DISTRESS; $2,500.00 FOR TREE TRIMMING; AND $883.00 FOR JUNK HAULING. ADDITIONALLY, THE COURT ALSO GRANTS PERMANTENT INJUCTION REQUIRING DEFENDANTS TO ABATE THE DECLARED PUBLIC NUISANCE PROPERTY AT 232 DOWNEY STREET, SAN FRANCISCO CA 94117. (A) ABATE PRIVATE NUISANCE AS IT RELATES TO PLAINTIFF'S PROPERTY. (B) PROHIBITS DEFENDANTS FROM DEGRADING THE NUISANCE PROPERTY. (C) PROHIBITS DEFENDANTS FROM PERFORMING WORK/RENOVATION ON THE NUISANCE PROPERTY WITHOUT FIRST OBTAINING REQUIRED PERMITS FROM ANY CITY AGENCY OR DEPARTMENT. (D) DEFENDANTS SHALL IMMEDIATELY SURRENDER COMPLETE POSSESSION OF THE NUISANCE PROPERTY. (E) DEFENDANTS SHALL FORWARD TO THE APPOINTED RECEIVER WITH TAX IDENTIFICATION NUMBER. (F) DEFENDANTS SHALL NOT INTERFERE IN ANY WAY WITH THE RECEIVER'S DUTIES AND OBLIGATIONS SET FORTH. THE NUISANCE PROPERTY SHALL BE SOLD. EXPENDITURES TO PREPARE THE REAL PROPERTY FOR SALE, INCLUDING NEW PAINT, NECESSARY REPAIRS, AND/OR IMPROVEMENTS, SHALL NOT EXCEED $100.000.00. THE RECEIVER SHALL BE COMPENSATED FOR HIS SERVICES AT THE RATES SET FORTH THE DECLARATION OF GERARD KEENA FILED IN CONJUCTION WITH THE MOTION FOR DEFAULT JUDGMENT, AND ONLY AFTER THE SALE OF THE NUISANCE PROPERTY. THE COURT SHALL RETAIN JURISDICTION TO CONFIRM THE SALE OF THE REAL PROPERTY AND TO REVIEW THE REPORT OF THE RECEIVER, AND TO ORDER EQUITABLE ADJUSTMENTS THAT THE COURT CONSIDERS JUST AND APPROPRIATE.

JAIME F. COUCHE, ESQ. WAS PRESENT WITH PLAINTIFF DANIEL L'ABBE, WHO WAS PLACED UNDER OATH, BUT DID NOT TESTIFY. GERARD F. KEENA, THE PROPOSED RECEIVER, WAS PLACED UNDER OATH BUT DID NOT TESTIFY. BOTH MESSRS. L'ABBE AND KEENA APPEARED VIA ZOOM FOR THIS MORNING'S PROCEEDINGS. NO APPEARANCE BY DEFENDANTS AERON NOWELL, ET AL. THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF L'ABBE, DANIEL RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT NOWELL, AERON AERON NOWELL AS THE ADMINSTRATOR OF THE ESTATE OF KAREN W HOKE $23,383.00 PRINCIPAL, $1,095.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $24,478.00, SEE SCANNED DOC - "PRINCIPAL" IS COMPRISED OF THE FOLLOWING: $20,000.00 FOR LOSS OF USE AND ENJOYMENT OF PLAINTIFF'S PROPERTY, AND RELATED EMOTIONAL AND MENTAL DISTRESS; $2,500.00 FOR TREE TRIMMING; AND $883.00 FOR JUNK HAULING. ADDITIONALLY, THE COURT ALSO GRANTS PERMANTENT INJUCTION REQUIRING DEFENDANTS TO ABATE THE DECLARED PUBLIC NUISANCE PROPERTY AT 232 DOWNEY STREET, SAN FRANCISCO CA 94117. (A) ABATE PRIVATE NUISANCE AS IT RELATES TO PLAINTIFF'S PROPERTY. (B) PROHIBITS DEFENDANTS FROM DEGRADING THE NUISANCE PROPERTY. (C) PROHIBITS DEFENDANTS FROM PERFORMING WORK/RENOVATION ON THE NUISANCE PROPERTY WITHOUT FIRST OBTAINING REQUIRED PERMITS FROM ANY CITY AGENCY OR DEPARTMENT. (D) DEFENDANTS SHALL IMMEDIATELY SURRENDER COMPLETE POSSESSION OF THE NUISANCE PROPERTY. (E) DEFENDANTS SHALL FORWARD TO THE APPOINTED RECEIVER WITH TAX IDENTIFICATION NUMBER. (F) DEFENDANTS SHALL NOT INTERFERE IN ANY WAY WITH THE RECEIVER'S DUTIES AND OBLIGATIONS SET FORTH. THE NUISANCE PROPERTY SHALL BE SOLD. EXPENDITURES TO PREPARE THE REAL PROPERTY FOR SALE, INCLUDING NEW PAINT, NECESSARY REPAIRS, AND/OR IMPROVEMENTS, SHALL NOT EXCEED $100.000.00. THE RECEIVER SHALL BE COMPENSATED FOR HIS SERVICES AT THE RATES SET FORTH THE DECLARATION OF GERARD KEENA FILED IN CONJUCTION WITH THE MOTION FOR DEFAULT JUDGMENT, AND ONLY AFTER THE SALE OF THE NUISANCE PROPERTY. THE COURT SHALL RETAIN JURISDICTION TO CONFIRM THE SALE OF THE REAL PROPERTY AND TO REVIEW THE REPORT OF THE RECEIVER, AND TO ORDER EQUITABLE ADJUSTMENTS THAT THE COURT CONSIDERS JUST AND APPROPRIATE.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-07-2020 Continued To Mar-30-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Plaintiff Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Default Judgment
Off Calendar Nov-16-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Aeron Nowell.
Dept: 514. Judge: Gerardo C Sandoval. Clerk: Lyssette Bareng. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.
Jamie Couche, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Daniel L'Abbe who was placed under oath and testified.
Gerard Keena of Bay Area Receivership Group, was placed under oath and testified.
All parties appeared via ZOOM Video Conference for this morning's proceedings.
No appearance by defendant Aeron Nowell.
Plaintiff's Complaint for Private Nuisance, Public Nuisance, Negligence and Injuction Relief was filed on September 6, 2019.
Amendment to Complaint to Substitute True Names for incorrect names (Aeron Nowell for Aeron Howell) filed on September 30, 2019.
Personal Service of plaintiff's Summons, Complaint and accompanying documents on defendant Aeron Nowell and Aeron Nowell as Administrator of the Estate of Karen W. Hoke on October 17, 2019.
Default as to both defendants Aeron Nowell and Aeron Nowell as Administrator of the Estate of Karen W. Hoke entered on January 8, 2020.
DOE defendants were dismissed without prejudice on September 9, 2020.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Daniel L'Abbe, and against defendants Aeron Nowell and Aeron Nowell as Administrator of the Estate of Karen W. Hoke, as follows:

Principal/Damages - *$23,383.00.
Prejudgment Interest - WAIVED.
Attorney Fees - RESERVED by the Court
Costs - $1,095.00.

Total - $24,478.00.

*$20,000.00 - For loss of use and enjoyment of plaintiff's property.
$3,383.00 - Tree Trimming and Junk Hauling.

Gerard Keena of Bay Area Receivership Group is appointed as the Court's receiver over the Nuisance Property with full powers under Code of Civil Procedure section 564 and subject to further orders of the Court.

The Court also GRANTS Permantent Injuction requiring defendants to abate the declared public nuisance property at 232 Downey Street, San Francisco CA 94117.
(a) Abate private nuisance as it relates to plaintiff's property.
(b) Prohibits defendants from degrading the Nuisance Property.
(c) Prohibits defendants from performing work/renovation on the Nuisance Property without first obtaining required permits from any City agency or department.
(d) Defendants shall immediately surrender complete possession of the Nuisance Property.
(e) Defendants shall forward to the Appointed Receiver with Tax Identification Number.
(f) Defendants shall not interfere in any way with the Receiver's duties and obligations set forth.

The Nuisance Property shall be sold. Expenditures to prepare the Real Property for sale, including new paint, necessary repairs, and/or improvements, shall not exceed $100.000.00.
The receiver shall be compensated for his services at the rates set forth the declaration of Gerard Keena filed in conjuction with the motion for default judgment, and only after the sale of the Nuisance Property.

The Court shall retain jurisdiction to confirm the sale of the Real Property and to review the report of the Receiver, and to order equitable adjustments that the Court considers just and appropriate.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare the proposed Interlocutory Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

Matter is CONTINUED to February 18, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 514 for a Progress Report on the sale of the property.

Notice Of Motion And Motion By Plaintiff Daniel Labbe To Compel Defendant Aeron Nowell To Respond To Discovery
Off Calendar Feb-28-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Deborah Goodman, Esq., 415-956-6413, appearing for Daniel L'Abbe, Plaintiff and moving party.

No other appearances.

Argued. Ms. Goodman stated that the defendant has defaulted and can not be notified, that she would like the court to grant her motion and cited CCP 1010. The Court questioned if counsel complied with the tentative ruling rule. Ms. Goodman responded, "No". The Court stated that it would not entertain any further ex parte communication.

The Court adopts the modified tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DANIEL L'ABBE's Motion To Compel Defendant Aeron Nowell To Respond To Discovery is OFF CALENDAR, plaintiff may move to have the motion reconsidered. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005 and CCP 1013. Additionally, default against Defendant Aeron Nowell entered on January 8, 2020. Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)
